original_abstract: Fires in smart cities represent a critical threat to urban infrastructure and public safety, necessitating rapid and reliable detection to prevent catastrophic loss. Traditional sensor-based methods often struggle with response times and accuracy, particularly in large-scale or outdoor environments. To address these limitations, this paper introduces the Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS), an improved real-time approach leveraging the state-of-the-art YOLOv8 deep learning algorithm. By focusing on the extraction of fire-specific visual features, the SFDS enhances detection precision and minimizes false alarms compared to conventional methods. The proposed architecture is integrated into a multi-layered smart city framework comprising IoT, Fog, Cloud, and Application layers. This structure facilitates low-latency data processing at the edge while utilizing cloud resources for extensive data management, ensuring a swift response to emergencies. Experimental results demonstrate that the SFDS achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching a high precision rate of 97.1% across all classes. Beyond fire safety, the system’s versatile design allows it to be adapted for monitoring other urban hazards such as flooding or gas leaks, making it a robust solution for intelligent security and forest fire management.
humanized_abstract: Since fire incidents in smart cities pose severe threats to urban physical objects and human life, timely and efficient fire detection is very important to prevent accidental losses. However, current fire detection approaches such as sensor technologies, particularly those based on computer vision using sensor devices, suffer from slow speed, poor detection accuracy and many limitations, especially in complex environments, such as large industrial areas and open environment scenarios. In order to promote the efficiency of fire detection and to enhance the detection accuracy, this study develops a Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS) based on real-time deep learning, which adopts advanced YOLOv8 to extract fire-related features and hence improves the detection accuracy and reduces the amount of false alarms to realize strong resistance to interference and to detect fires in complex backgrounds. The SFDS is realized in the smart city multi-layer architecture based on the IoT, Fog, Cloud and Application layer, and it strengthens the timely processing of data at the terminal end and makes full use of the functions of cloud computing. This allows for efficient processing of data for rapid processing of fire incidents. The experiments show that the detection results for the proposed system have reached an advanced level, with the overall detection accuracy for the four kinds of object detection reaching 97.1% or more. Finally, the extensibility of SFDS was verified, and it was stated that the system could be used to realise intelligent fire protection systems and enhanced forest fire prevention in smart cities. It can also be used to forecast floods, detect leakages of natural gas and other applications, in order to realize the intelligent security service in smart cities.
